notes.anchor: What I am after is a frequented spot
notes.note: Celebritas --- a much-visited, public location --- is precisely what Cicero wants for Tullia's shrine, so that her memory will stay before the eyes of the city. This is why a cramped plot at busy Ostia could serve where a secluded estate would not.

notes.anchor: where he is waiting for Pansa
notes.note: Marcus Brutus was governor of Cisalpine Gaul in 46--45 BC and was expected to hand the province over to his successor Vibius Pansa. Cicero, dreading a visit from Brutus on his way back to Rome, is tracking his movements so he can contrive an excuse to avoid the meeting in his grief.
